title: DSDS Best Practices Guideline
description: Documents actionable usage guidance for an artifact. Each item is a self-contained rule paired with a rationale explaining why. Best practices tell the reader what to do (or not do) and why. They appear alongside other guideline types in the unified guidelines array on any artifact.

bestPracticeEntry.description: A single, self-contained piece of usage guidance. Every best practice pairs an actionable statement with a rationale. `kind` classifies enforcement. `category` groups by discipline. `target` identifies which anatomy part or content area the guidance applies to. `criteria` links to external standards (WCAG, platform guidelines, etc.).

guidance.description: The actionable guidance statement. MUST be concrete and unambiguous. Avoid subjective language like 'use sparingly' or 'when possible'.

rationale.description: Why this guidance exists. Cite evidence, accessibility standards, or user research when available. MUST NOT restate the guidance.

kind.description: The enforcement level, classifying how strictly the best practice should be followed. Values align with RFC 2119: 'required' (MUST — non-compliance is a defect), 'encouraged' (SHOULD — follow in most cases; exceptions need justification), 'informational' (MAY — advisory with no enforcement expectation), 'discouraged' (SHOULD NOT — avoid unless justified), 'prohibited' (MUST NOT — violations are defects). Defaults to 'informational'.

category.description: The discipline or domain this best practice belongs to. Used to group best practices by concern. Standard values: 'visual-design' (color, typography, spacing, layout), 'interaction' (behavior, animation, transitions, gestures), 'accessibility' (inclusive design, assistive technology, WCAG compliance), 'content' (text, imagery, tone, voice, labeling), 'motion' (animation timing, easing, reduced-motion), 'development' (implementation patterns, performance, code conventions). Custom values are permitted and SHOULD be lowercase kebab-case.

target.description: The anatomy part or content area this best practice applies to (e.g., 'label', 'icon', 'helper-text'). When omitted, the best practice applies to the artifact as a whole.

criteria.description: References to external standards that this best practice addresses. Each entry carries a URL and a human-readable label. For WCAG criteria, use the direct URL to the success criterion and a descriptive label (e.g., '1.4.3 Contrast (Minimum)').

url.description: URL to the external standard or requirement (e.g., 'https://www.w3.org/TR/WCAG22/#contrast-minimum').

label.description: A human-readable label for the criterion (e.g., '1.4.3 Contrast (Minimum)', 'WCAG 2.5.8 Target Size'). When omitted, tools MAY derive a label from the URL.

examples.description: Examples showing encouraged and discouraged approaches.

tags.description: Freeform keywords to relate best practices across categories and disciplines (e.g., 'rtl', 'localization', 'validation', 'contrast').

bestPractices.description: Documents actionable usage rules for an artifact. Each item pairs a guidance statement with a rationale. Categorize by discipline, target specific anatomy parts, and link to external standards like WCAG. Best practices complement other guideline types — use-cases tell the reader *when* to use the artifact, best practices tell the reader *how* to use it correctly.

items.description: The best practice rules. Each item is a guidance statement paired with a rationale. Ordering is significant — tools SHOULD display best practices in this order. Authors SHOULD order by importance or group by category.

kind.description: Identifies this guideline as a best practices spec.
